questions.question: A car travels 120 m in 8 s. Calculate its speed.
questions.answer: Speed = distance / time = 120 / 8 = 15 m/s.

questions.question: Convert 72 km/h into m/s.
A train starts from rest and reaches 15 m/s in 5 s. Calculate acceleration.
A car slows down from 25 m/s to 5 m/s in 10 s. Find acceleration.
Write the three equations of motion.
questions.answer: v = u + at, s = ut + ½at², v² − u² = 2as.

questions.question: Explain the condition required for applying equations of motion.
questions.answer: The equations of motion are valid only for motion in a straight line with uniform acceleration.

questions.question: Why does a body moving in a circular path experience acceleration?
questions.answer: Because the direction of velocity changes continuously in circular motion.

questions.question: Explain why velocity changes in circular motion even when speed remains constant.
questions.answer: Velocity changes because direction changes continuously.

questions.question: Explain how speed is determined from a distance-time graph.
questions.answer: Speed is determined by calculating the slope of the distance-time graph.

questions.question: Explain how displacement can be determined from a velocity-time graph.
questions.answer: Displacement equals the area under the velocity-time graph.

questions.question: Why is acceleration zero when velocity remains constant?
questions.answer: Because acceleration depends on change in velocity, and constant velocity means no change.

questions.question: Explain why a freely falling body has uniform acceleration.
questions.answer: Because gravity provides constant acceleration towards the Earth.

questions.question: A body moves with velocity 12 m/s for 4 s. Calculate displacement.
questions.answer: Displacement = velocity × time = 12 × 4 = 48 m.

questions.question: Explain the meaning of negative acceleration.
questions.answer: Negative acceleration means velocity decreases with time and is also called retardation.

questions.question: What will be the displacement after completing one full round of a circular track?
questions.answer: Zero because the initial and final positions are the same.

questions.question: Why is velocity-time graph important in studying motion?
questions.answer: It helps determine acceleration from slope and displacement from the area under the graph.

questions.question: Explain the difference between speed and velocity.
questions.answer: Speed is a scalar quantity while velocity is a vector quantity that includes direction.

questions.question: Why does circular motion involve acceleration?
questions.answer: Because the direction of velocity keeps changing.

questions.question: What type of motion is shown by a satellite revolving around Earth?
questions.answer: Uniform circular motion.

questions.question: Explain why motion is considered relative.
questions.answer: Because an object may appear moving with respect to one reference point but stationary with respect to another.

questions.question: Which instrument measures speed of vehicles?
questions.answer: Speedometer.

questions.question: Which instrument measures the distance travelled by a vehicle?
questions.answer: Odometer.

questions.question: What happens to velocity when acceleration is positive?
questions.answer: Velocity increases with time.

questions.question: Give two examples of uniform circular motion.
questions.answer: Motion of Earth around the Sun and motion of a satellite around Earth.

questions.question: Why is direction important in velocity but not in speed?
questions.answer: Velocity includes direction while speed depends only on magnitude.
